acceptance_targets: [CH05_OVEREXPLAINED_AFTERMATH] Pass if the revised aftermath from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:91 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:115, at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:191, from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:217 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:220, and from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:415 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:416 leaves the clues legible through dialogue and behavior, and at least two of those aftermath passages now introduce a new decision, new uncertainty, or a concrete next move rather than paraphrasing the clue that just landed. Fail if the chapter still immediately translates the interviews into thesis language the reader already has.

[CH05_STUDY_INFERENCE_UNDERPROVED] Pass if a cold reader can point to a detail from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:161 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:173 that could only come from Cliff entering the study recently, or else can see in the follow-up from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:219 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:220 and from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:415 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:416 that Mallick is treating the beat as suspicion rather than proof. Fail if Cliff still only demonstrates ordinary familiarity that a frequent guest could plausibly have.

[CH05_FORENSIC_TOX_LEAP] Pass if each requested forensic step at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:419 is visibly motivated by on-page evidence from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:407 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:419. If 'tox moved up' remains, the revised text must name the clue that makes chemistry relevant now; otherwise that request must be absent. Fail if toxicology still arrives as unexplained detective intuition.

[CH05_CLIPPED_NARRATION_PATTERN] Pass if the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:143, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:165, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:219, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:221, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:235, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:415 contains only grammatically complete narrative sentences. Fail if any cited spot still relies on subject-dropped fragments such as 'Not just knew it' or 'Head bent toward the water.'
[RED-07] Ch 5 no longer contains a 'twenty years' reference to Joel's career or a standalone sentence identifying him as a public defender.
[RED-14] Ch 5 contains Paul's employer name and no more than 1 additional insurance-metaphor label.
[RED-16] Ch 5 mentions Mallick's reading glasses no more than twice.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised aftermath from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:91 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:115, at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:191, from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:217 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:220, and from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:415 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:416 leaves the clues legible through dialogue and behavior, and at least two of those aftermath passages now introduce a new decision, new uncertainty, or a concrete next move rather than paraphrasing the clue that just landed. Fail if the chapter still immediately translates the interviews into thesis language the reader already has.
findings.problem: The chapter keeps translating clues into explicit case-note conclusions after the interviews have already made those conclusions legible. That repeated paraphrase lowers reader participation and keeps the discovery engine from feeling as sharp and self-confident as the material wants to be.
findings.rewrite_direction: From snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:91 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:115, then again at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:191, from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:217 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:220, and from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:415 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:416, cut or recast the explanatory aftermath so each beat adds new pressure, uncertainty, or action instead of restating what the dialogue already proved. Let Paul's too-fast 'I went back to bed,' Cliff's study correction, and Tommy's door contradiction do more of the chapter's work before Mallick sums them up.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can point to a detail from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:161 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:173 that could only come from Cliff entering the study recently, or else can see in the follow-up from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:219 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:220 and from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:415 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:416 that Mallick is treating the beat as suspicion rather than proof. Fail if Cliff still only demonstrates ordinary familiarity that a frequent guest could plausibly have.
findings.problem: Cliff's correction about the study layout does not, by itself, prove fresh post-death entry into the room. A longtime friend who had done business there could plausibly know that layout already, so the chapter's later treatment of this beat as evidence of study traffic is stronger than the on-page proof supports.
findings.rewrite_direction: From snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:161 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:173, then in the follow-up from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:219 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:220 or from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:415 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:416, either make Cliff reveal a detail only a recent entrant would know (for example the thumb-latch, the lake print, a desk-paper arrangement, or another fresh room-state detail) or downgrade Mallick's conclusion from effective certainty to a suspicion she still needs to test.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if each requested forensic step at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:419 is visibly motivated by on-page evidence from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:407 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:419. If 'tox moved up' remains, the revised text must name the clue that makes chemistry relevant now; otherwise that request must be absent. Fail if toxicology still arrives as unexplained detective intuition.
findings.problem: The closing request to move toxicology up is not operationally legible from the clue chain visible in this chapter. Sternum photos and bedding follow from the CPR contradiction, but the page does not show what fresh fact makes Mallick prioritize a chemical question at this exact moment.
findings.rewrite_direction: From snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:407 to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:419, add the missing causal bridge between the interviews and the toxicology request, or limit the medical-examiner call to sternum photographs and bedding until the chapter has surfaced a concrete chemical clue.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:143, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:165, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:219, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:221, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:235, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:415 contains only grammatically complete narrative sentences. Fail if any cited spot still relies on subject-dropped fragments such as 'Not just knew it' or 'Head bent toward the water.'
findings.problem: The narration repeatedly relies on clipped fragments and subject-dropped sentences ('Not fake. Practiced.' / 'Looked up.' / 'Not just knew it.'), which violates the constitution's no-elliptical-clipping rule and makes Mallick's private register feel mannered instead of precise.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:143, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:165, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:219, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:221, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:235, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_05.md:415, replace the clipped fragments with short complete sentences. Keep the velocity and compression, but restore subjects and auxiliaries so Mallick's private voice reads as controlled rather than syntactically dropped.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 5 no longer contains a 'twenty years' reference to Joel's career or a standalone sentence identifying him as a public defender.
findings.evidence: Ch 4 line 986: 'In twenty years as a public defender in Hennepin County, he'd never once considered that side of the room.' Ch 5 line 1358: 'twenty years in arraignment rooms and interview rooms had given him a feel for what secrecy did to air.' Ch 5 line 1381: 'A public defender who knew exactly how much silence was worth.'
findings.problem: Joel's 'twenty years as a public defender' is stated in Ch 4 (line 986) and restated in near-identical framing in Ch 5 (line 1358) — adjacent chapters. Ch 5 also adds a second 'public defender' label at line 1381.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch 5 line 1358, cut 'twenty years.' Joel can observe secrecy in the room without restating tenure. At line 1381, Mallick's shorthand 'the lawyer' suffices — cut the standalone public-defender label.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 5 contains Paul's employer name and no more than 1 additional insurance-metaphor label.
findings.evidence: Ch 5 lines 1379, 1391-1395, 1467, 1479, 1507: 'the insurance man' (1379); 'Claims adjuster... Mutual of Omaha, but the Minneapolis office' (1393); 'insurance precision' (1467); 'like claims work, isn't it?' (1479); 'sat like he was at a claims desk' (1507). Five insurance-adjuster labels for Paul in one chapter, after Ch 2 line 753 and Ch 4 lines 1092-1100 already proved this baseline.
findings.problem: Paul's insurance-adjuster identity is re-proved 5 times in Ch 5 on top of the 3-4 times it was proved in Chs 2 and 4. The specific employer (Mutual of Omaha) at line 1393 is new information; the rest are pure baseline re-proving. 'Insurance precision' and 'claims desk' and 'claims work' all say the same thing.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the Mutual of Omaha detail (1393, new info) and one behavioral observation. Cut 'insurance precision' (1467), 'like claims work' (1479), and 'claims desk' (1507) — these are three versions of the same metaphor in one chapter.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 5 mentions Mallick's reading glasses no more than twice.
findings.evidence: Ch 5 lines 1379, 1591, 1651, 1874, 1880, 1974: Mallick's reading glasses are mentioned 6 times in Ch 5: 'reading glasses pushed to the end of her nose' (1379); 'reading glasses pushed up on her forehead' (1591); 'over her glasses' (1651); 'reading glasses were on the beaded chain' (1874); 'put her reading glasses on' (1880); 'took off her reading glasses and let them hang on the beaded chain' (1974).
findings.problem: Mallick's reading-glasses-on-beaded-chain prop is introduced and then cycled through 6 on/off/push/hang actions in a single chapter. This establishes the glasses as a re-introduction tag rather than a meaningful character detail. The same on/off cycle repeats in Chs 7 and 10.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce glasses mentions in Ch 5 to 2: the introduction (1379) and one active use. Cut the remaining 4 on/off business actions. The glasses can exist without being an every-scene prop.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: The comedy is the creative risk and the creative achievement. These are men in their late forties who talk the way men in their late forties talk when there are no women present and the drinks have been flowing for hours: crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit, casually cruel, and occasionally accidentally beautiful. Do not clean this up. Do not soften the language. Do not make them witty where they would be vulgar. Do not make them eloquent where they would be clumsy. The profanity is not decoration — it is the texture of the friendship. When the second death breaks the comic register, the absence of that profane warmth should feel like a loss, not a relief. The murder, when it arrives, should be rendered with physical specificity — the water, the struggle, the sounds. The grief should be specific. The shame should be specific. The twenty-two-year-old guilt should be embodied, not summarized. If a scene makes the writer uncomfortable, that is a signal to stay in it longer, not to cut away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Every euphemism for death, sex, bodily function, emotional state, or financial desperation must be replaced with the direct term or a character-specific avoidance that itself reveals something about the character. Cliff saying 'restructure' when he means 'I'm broke' is character work. The narrator describing Cliff as 'facing financial challenges' is sanitization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the absolute norm. These are informal men in their late forties who have been friends for twenty-five years. Uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate emphasis, Joel's occasional legal precision, or Mallick's public-persona overcorrection. If two consecutive lines of dialogue contain uncontracted forms and neither character has a stated reason for formality, at least one is wrong.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. After a dialogue-heavy chapter, lead the next with interiority or description. After an interview chapter, give the reader a memory or a quiet observation. After a comic set piece, let the next chapter breathe. The scene counts vary (2-3) and should feel varied — a two-scene chapter after a three-scene chapter creates natural pacing shift. The tonal break at Chapter 14 should feel like a gear change, not a gradual dimming.
prose_style_profile.diction: Muscular, specific, unpretentious. No literary posturing. The prose should sound like intelligent conversation, not workshopped fiction. Prefer concrete nouns over abstract ones. Prefer active constructions. Avoid decorative metaphor — when a metaphor appears, it should be load-bearing. The comedy demands precision: funny is specific, and vague is never funny.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Prestige-literary inflation — do not elevate the comic register into literary solemnity. A man flushing Viagra while a detective knocks on the door is funny. The prose should be funny. Do not describe it as if it were a scene from Dostoevsky.

Comedic exhaustion — do not let every paragraph contain a joke. Comedy needs rhythm, which means it needs non-comic beats to land against. Let some paragraphs be straightforwardly descriptive or emotional.
Interior monologue sprawl — close third is not stream of consciousness. The POV character's interiority should be selective and purposeful, not an open channel of every thought.
Simultaneous narration — do not describe characters 'simultaneously' feeling multiple things. Choose the dominant feeling and let the undertone be implicit.
Forensic procedure porn — this is not a police procedural. Keep investigation details specific but brief. The comedy and character are the vehicle, not the forensics.

Metaphor creep — do not let the same metaphor template appear more than twice in a chapter. If you've used a sports metaphor, a water metaphor, and a fire metaphor, check that none of them are doing the same structural work.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV. Each chapter is locked to one character's interiority. No omniscient intrusions. The prose should take on the coloring of the POV character without becoming parody — Nate's chapters move faster and list more, Doug's chapters are quieter and more observational, Mallick's chapters shift between her two registers.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Conversational propulsion, not literary drift. The prose should move like someone telling you this story — not rushed, but never self-admiring. Vary sentence length aggressively: long compound sentences for the comic set pieces, short declarative hits for the turns. The humor lives in rhythm as much as content — the timing of a punchline is a prose-level decision.
sensory_bias: auditory — conversations, lake sounds, house creaks, the specific silence of a house where someone died
proprioceptive — hangovers, physical discomfort, adrenaline, the specific sensation of a body that hasn't slept
olfactory — booze, lake water, morning coffee, the basement must, the casserole
